page.content: In the context of Adobe Systems' Enterprise license revenue metrics from 2020 to 2022, the data reveals significant insights.

lines.text: In the context of Adobe Systems' Enterprise license revenue metrics from 2020 to 2022, the data reveals significant insights.

page.content: For the year 2020, the Licenses Sold totaled 1,200 units, generating a Volume of Sales of €150M with 300 New Clients and an Average Revenue per Client of €125, while Existing Clients amounted to 900, contributing €120M in License Revenue. By 2021, Licenses Sold increased to 1,500 units, with a Volume of Sales of €145M, New Clients rising to 400 and an Average Revenue per Client of €130, alongside 1,100 Existing Clients generating €145M in License Revenue.

lines.text: For the year 2020, the Licenses Sold totaled 1,200 units, generating a Volume of Sales of €150M with 300 New Clients and an
Average Revenue per Client of €125, while Existing Clients amounted to 900, contributing €120M in License Revenue. By
2021, Licenses Sold increased to 1,500 units, with a Volume of Sales of €145M, New Clients rising to 400 and an Average
Revenue per Client of €130, alongside 1,100 Existing Clients generating €145M in License Revenue.

page.content: In 2022, Licenses Sold reached 1,800 units, with a Volume of Sales of €150M, New Clients at 500, and an Average Revenue per Client of €135, as Existing Clients grew to 1,300, yielding €150M in License Revenue, reflecting a Total Revenue Growth of 12%. Regarding SaaS recurring revenue, the Monthly Recurring Revenue was €20M in 2020, growing to €25M in 2021 and €30M in 2022, with Annual Recurring Revenue progressing from €240M to €300M and €360M respectively. The Churn Rate decreased from 5.2% to 4.8% and finally to 4.5%, while the Customer Acquisition Cost reduced from €150 to €140 and then to €130, indicating improved efficiency in client acquisition.
